~~~
<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.Begin -->
<!--
    # This information is used for caching.
    [PlutoStaticHTML.State]
    input_sha = "8af2c93ed770c21785bcdc484f80f7ed1f7b1c2f43e2d99d312a409a380286be"
    julia_version = "1.12.5"
-->

<div class="markdown"><h1 id="Solving-Kinematic-Constraints">Solving Kinematic Constraints</h1><p><img alt="Reference frames for door opening" src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cooperrc/dynamics/refs/heads/main/images/door-frames.png"/></p><p>Moving door and the reference frames to define its motion in body-fixed and global coordinates. </p><p><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=u27rJ7I0d3g &quot;Solving for door opening kinematics - click to watch&quot;"><img alt="Solving for door opening kinematics" src="https://img.youtube.com/vi/u27rJ7I0d3g/0.jpg"/></a></p><h2 id="Door-Example-Setup">Door Example Setup</h2><p>A door is modeled as a rigid body with center of mass at <span class="tex">\(R_1\)</span> and rotation <span class="tex">\(\theta_1\)</span>. A point such as the handle is located using offsets <span class="tex">\((x_H, y_H)\)</span>: <span class="tex">\(R_H = R_1 + x_H\,\mathbf{i}_1 + y_H\,\mathbf{j}_1\)</span></p><p>The hinge point, offset by door width <span class="tex">\(W\)</span> and thickness <span class="tex">\(T\)</span>: <span class="tex">\(R_{hinge}^{(1)} = R_1 - \frac{W}{2}\mathbf{i}_1 + \frac{T}{2}\mathbf{j}_1\)</span></p><p>The same hinge point expressed in ground coordinates: <span class="tex">\(R_{hinge}^{(0)} = R_0 + M_x\mathbf{i}_0 + M_y\mathbf{j}_0\)</span></p><p>We use these hinge constraints to build <span class="tex">\(\mathbf{C}(\mathbf{q},~t)\)</span></p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>using NonlinearSolve,Plots, Latexify</code></pre>



<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Time‑Varying-Constraint">Time‑Varying Constraint</h2><p>If the door is pushed upward at constant speed: <span class="tex">\(R_{1y} = 2t \quad \Rightarrow \quad \dot{R}_{1y} = 2\)</span> This creates a third constraint to be incorporated into the system</p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
    #q = [x_1, y_1, θ_1]
    W = 1
    T = 3e-2
    mx = 0.0
    my = T/2
    C(q, p) = [q[1] - W/2*cos(q[3]) - T/2*sin(q[3]) - mx,
               q[2] - W/2*sin(q[3]) + T/2*cos(q[3]) - my,
               q[2] - 2*p - T/2]
               
end</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-T">C (generic function with 1 method)</pre>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
    time = LinRange(0, 0.25, 51)
    q_all = zeros(length(time), 3)
    for i in range(1, length(time))
        
        prob = NonlinearProblem(C, [0, 0, 0], time[i])
        sol = solve(prob)
        q_all[i, :] = sol
    end
end</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>plot(time, q_all)</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"/>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>C(q_all[51, :], time[51])</code></pre>
<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-hash126247">3-element Vector{Float64}:
 5.204170427930421e-18
 6.609296443471635e-16
 1.3877787807814457e-17</pre>


<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Solving-the-System-speeds">Solving the System speeds</h2><p>At each timestep:</p><ol><li><p><strong>Position solve:</strong> find <span class="tex">\(q\)</span> such that <span class="tex">\(C(q,t)=0\)</span></p></li><li><p><strong>Velocity solve:</strong> solve linear system <span class="tex">\(C_q \dot{q} = -C_t\)</span></p></li></ol></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>begin
    dCdq(q, p) = [1 0 W/2*sin(q[3])-T/2*cos(q[3]);
              0 1 -W/2*cos(q[3])-T/2*sin(q[3]);
             0 1 0]
    Ct(q, p) = [0,0,2.0]
    dq_all = zeros(length(time), 3)
    for i in range(1, length(time))
        qt = q_all[i, :]
        sol = dCdq(qt, time[i])\Ct(qt, time[i])
        dq_all[i, :] = sol
    end
end</code></pre>


<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>plot(time, dq_all)</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"/>


<pre class="code-output documenter-example-output" id="var-plot_oriented_rectangle!">plot_oriented_rectangle! (generic function with 1 method)</pre>


<div class="markdown"><h2 id="Animate-the-door-motion">Animate the door motion</h2><p>In this next step, we use <code>plot_oriented_rectangle</code> to show the door in a given position and orientation. The input to the Julia Assistant in copilot was, </p><blockquote><p>"I need you to give me a julia function that plots a rectangle given its x,y position and orientation, theta in a vector q=[x, y, theta] and let me define its width and thickness for the plot"</p></blockquote><p>Then, we use the <code>@gif</code> loop to watch the door open over time. </p></div>

<pre class='language-julia'><code class='language-julia'>@gif for i in 1:length(time)
    pose_q = q_all[i, :]
    
    base_plot = plot(xlim = (-0.2, 1.5), ylim = (-0.5, 1), aspect_ratio = :equal)
    plot_oriented_rectangle!(pose_q, W, T; face_color = :magenta, edge_color = :black, face_alpha = 0.7)
end</code></pre>
<img src="data:image/gif;base64,"/>



<div class='manifest-versions'>
<p>Built with Julia 1.12.5 and</p>
Latexify 0.16.10<br>
NonlinearSolve 4.16.0<br>
Plots 1.41.6
</div>

<!-- PlutoStaticHTML.End -->
~~~

[Pluto.jl](https://plutojl.org)._
